correct_answer: The model answer provides a complete solution to the three-part physics problem involving a pendulum with force sensor data and magnetic field effects. Key results: (1) Period T = 4π/7 s ≈ 1.80 s, length L = 0.800 m. (2) Mass m = 0.0799 kg, maximum speed v_m = 0.142 m/s. (3) Magnetic field B = 0.0493 T or 0.0495 T, direction perpendicular into the page.

grading_points.description: Identify half-period from graph: T/2 = 2π/7 s from A→B→C motion.
Calculate full period: T = 4π/7 s ≈ 1.80 s.
Apply pendulum formula T = 2π√(L/g) to find L.
Set up force equation at endpoint: F_min = mg cosθ.
Set up force equation at lowest point: F_max - mg = mv_m²/L.
Apply energy conservation: mgL(1 - cosθ) = ½mv_m².
Solve system to find m = 0.0799 kg.
Calculate v_m = 0.142 m/s from energy or force equation.
State that Lorentz force does no work, so speed unchanged.
Analyze force directions for two passes through lowest point.
Compare F′₁ and F′₂ with F_max to deduce Lorentz force effects.
Determine magnetic field direction (into page) using left-hand rule.
Write equations: F′₁ = mg + mv_m²/L + qv_mB, F′₂ = mg + mv_m²/L - qv_mB.
